Your Lineup All Teams
Vol. 3 · No. 095 A Daily Dispatch from the Friendly Confines & Beyond Est. 2024

TheMorning Lineup

SunApr 5, 2026 Cubs6–6 · 5th NL Central Data: MLB Stats API
The ColumnistsThree takes on today
Walt KieniewiczThe Straight BeatRead

The Cubs beat the Rays 6-2 yesterday, and that’s what the box score says. It’s the kind of win that makes you scratch your head, because the Cubs have been this close to a breakthrough all season. They’ve been good enough to win, but not good enough to win consistently. This game, though, felt different. Not because of anything flashy, but because of the way the offense clicked and the pitching staff held serve.

The bats, for once, didn’t waste their chances. They didn’t just string together hits—they carved out runs. The lineup, which has spent so much time in the weeds,

Beatrice 'Bee' VittoriniThe OptimistRead

The Cubs didn’t just beat the Rays yesterday—they reminded us why we keep coming back, even when the standings don’t look so pretty. Six runs, two innings, and a bunch of folks in the stands who knew exactly how to cheer for a team that’s still finding its rhythm. You know, the kind of day where the ivy on Wrigley’s walls seems to sway in time with the crowd, like it’s part of the game itself.

Let’s talk about Crow-Armstrong. That kid’s got a pitcher’s arm and a hitter’s heart. I’ve seen him glide to that

Tony GedeskiThe PessimistRead

The Cubs won yesterday. That’s the first thing I’ll say. Six to two over the Rays, a team that’s been a dumpster fire all season. But here’s the thing: they’re 6-6, 2.0 games behind in the NL Central. You spend a billion on the marquee and forget to build a bullpen. Every Cubs summer is the same jazz and the same hangover.

The win was nice, but it’s the kind of victory that feels like a mirage. You look at the box score, and there’s a decent amount of runs scored, but the pitching

Key Plays

  • T1Nico Hoerner homers (1) on a fly ball to left field.
  • B3Jonathan Aranda out on a sacrifice fly to left fielder Michael Conforto. Jonny DeLuca scores.
  • T5Michael Conforto doubles (1) on a fly ball to right fielder Jake Fraley. Carson Kelly scores. Moisés Ballesteros scores. Dansby Swanson scores. Michael Conforto to 3rd. Michael Conforto advances to 3rd, on a throwing error by right fielder Jake Fraley. Michael Conforto advances to 3rd, on a throwing error by first baseman Jonathan Aranda.
  • T5Nico Hoerner doubles (6) on a ground ball to right fielder Jake Fraley. Michael Conforto scores.
  • T5Michael Busch reaches on a throwing error by catcher Hunter Feduccia. Nico Hoerner scores. Michael Busch to 2nd.
  • B8Jake Fraley singles on a line drive to left fielder Michael Conforto. Jonathan Aranda scores. Jake Fraley to 2nd.

04 The Pressbox Roster · Transactions ▸

Recent Transactions

04-06RecalledChicago Cubs recalled RHP Javier Assad from Iowa Cubs.
04-06ReturnedRHP Ethan Roberts returned to Iowa Cubs from Chicago Cubs.
04-05RecalledChicago Cubs recalled LHP Riley Martin from Iowa Cubs.
04-05RecalledChicago Cubs recalled RHP Ethan Roberts from Iowa Cubs.
04-04Status ChangeChicago Cubs placed RHP Cade Horton on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 4, 2026. Right forearm strain.
04-03AssignedChicago Cubs sent RF Seiya Suzuki on a rehab assignment to Knoxville Smokies.
04-03Status ChangeChicago Cubs placed LHP Matthew Boyd on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 3, 2026. Left biceps strain.

09 This Day in Cubs History Apr 5 ▸
  • 1916The Cubs play their first game at Weeghman Park, the stadium that would become Wrigley Field.
